issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close a dropdown panel change the panel’s hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in _openInstance and setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the on/off billing‑period switch toggles the period but never updates aria‑pressed on the button, leaving its pressed state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')) or toggle the attribute accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation hamburger button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button’s aria‑expanded state is updated, but its aria‑pressed state is never changed, so users cannot tell whether the menu is currently pressed.
issuesFound.fix: In toggle(), after calling open() or close(), set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open)) to reflect the button’s state.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated, but the inline validator only adds an error message; it never sets aria‑invalid on the input, so screen readers do not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult(), add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when result.valid is false (and remove it when valid).

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form fields do not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs are bound to the inline validator, but the validator never sets aria‑invalid on the fields, leaving errors invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Modify inlineValidator._renderResult() to set aria‑invalid to 'true' for invalid fields and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the panel is shown, but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to true; it is only set to false on close. This breaks the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') before animating the panel.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The number of visible products is updated in the UI, but no aria‑live region is used to announce the new count, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the filter result.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches) call liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`) or create a hidden <div role="status"> to receive the announcement.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan‑selection buttons do not announce the selected plan
issuesFound.explanation: Selecting a plan adds a CSS class but does not provide any live‑region feedback, so users cannot hear which plan is currently highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: After adding the featured class, call liveRegion.announce(`Plan ${planId} selected`) or update a hidden status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list updates are not announced when the list is hidden (no results)
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list becomes empty, the status element’s text is set, but the element lacks role="status"/aria‑live, so the “no results” message is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure _ statusEl has role="status" and aria‑live="polite" (or use liveRegion.announce) when count === 0.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. This meets the 24px minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for pointer inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the accessibility utility pattern, potentially causing content to be visible to all users or improperly positioned.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384-393: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on buttons missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 846). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 847: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on primary button hover missing override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-1px) without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the movement for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 871: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on hero image missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector defines a transition on transform and applies a 3D transform effect. The associated @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 929 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 929-932: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on feature cards missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card class transitions transform and applies translateY(-2px) on hover. The existing prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 1013 only targets the link arrow, not the card lift animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 993: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on featured pricing card missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) but is not covered by the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1184, which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 1188: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 136: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses color: #ffffff on background-color: #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of 3.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (16px semibold).
issuesFound.fix: Line 852: Change to `background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) which provides 4.8:1 contrast, or darken the primary brand color.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-outline class uses color: #5588bb (var(--colour-brand-primary)) on a transparent background (inheriting white), resulting in a 3.2:1 contrast ratio, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 881: Change to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa) which provides 4.8:1 contrast against white.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on ghost button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-ghost class uses color: #5588bb on transparent/white background, resulting in 3.2:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: Change to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses color: #ffffff on background-color: #ee9999, resulting in a 1.4:1 contrast ratio, which fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 903: Change to `background-color: var(--colour-error-dark);` (#991b1b) which provides 7.0:1 contrast with white text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step number badge
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-number uses color: #ffffff on a gradient background ranging from #5588bb to #bb88ee. The contrast against the lighter purple (#bb88ee) is approximately 1.8:1, failing the 3:1 requirement for large text (20px extrabold).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1046: Add `text-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);` or change to `color: var(--colour-neutral-900);` with appropriate background adjustment.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat values
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value uses color: #ffffff on a gradient background (similar to step-number) with semi-transparent overlay, resulting in insufficient contrast against the light background.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: Add `text-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);` or ensure background is darkened to meet 3:1 for large text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on the light gradient background, resulting in very poor contrast (approximately 1.5:1), making the text nearly invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1092: Change to `color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);` or `color: var(--colour-neutral-0);` with darker background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active filter tab
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button[aria-pressed="true"] uses color: #5588bb on background-color: #ffffff, resulting in 3.2:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA for normal text (14px medium).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: Change to `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (#004aaa).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] uses color: #ffffff on background-color: #5588bb, resulting in 3.2:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section text
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section and its child elements use white and semi-transparent white text on a light gradient background (similar to stats section), resulting in insufficient contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Add `background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary);` as a solid fallback, or darken the gradient colors. For text: ensure `color: var(--colour-neutral-0);` with `text-shadow` or sufficient background darkness.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter form labels

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hint text
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint uses color: #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) on background-color: #ffffff, resulting in a 2.7:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer text

Users with color vision deficiency cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by removing or adding the hidden attribute, but it never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button to indicate the state change. Assistive technologies cannot announce whether the submenu is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox, but the _open and _close functions never toggle aria-expanded on the input element. Screen-reader users are not informed when the suggestion list appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _open(): add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line _close(): add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid helper links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen-reader users are not informed that the field contains an invalid entry.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid(): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the markValid helper removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to treat the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid(): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation initializes, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to indicate that the closed drawer is initially collapsed. Assistive technologies assume the state is unknown.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after `_initialised = true;` and trigger event listener attachment, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is created without an initial aria-expanded attribute. Because the suggestion list is closed by default, the input should declare aria-expanded="false" so screen readers know the list is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's id. Screen-reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');`), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Although focus may move, users benefit from confirmation that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` before emitting the custom event.
  Line close(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before emitting the custom event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes the visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen-reader users may not realize the submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened');` after opening the panel.
  Line _closeInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');` after closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the billing period updates the displayed prices, but screen-reader users are not notified that the prices have changed or what the new period is.
issuesFound.fix:
Line setPeriod(): after updating the plan cards, add:
  `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item changes the visible content but does not announce the action or the item title to screen readers, leaving users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open(): add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));` after setting the expanded state.
  Line close(): add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));` after setting the collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing-period toggle uses role="switch" but never updates aria-checked to reflect the current state (month vs. year). Screen readers cannot convey whether the switch is on or off.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _onToggleClick(): after calculating `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, next === 'year');`
  Line _syncToggleState(): if using the simple toggle, ensure `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, period === 'year');` is called.

fpTitles: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced via live region
